questions.question: Solve the unconstrained optimization problem: minimize f(x) = x^2 + 2x - 3 subject to x >= 0. What is the optimal value of x? (Round your answer to 2 decimal places)
questions.hint: Use the method of Lagrange multipliers or calculus to solve the problem.

questions.question: Consider a quadratic function f(x) = -2x^2 + 3x + 1. Find the values of x for which f(x) is minimized by finding the vertex of the parabola. What is the minimum value of f(x)?
questions.hint: Use the formula for the x-coordinate of the vertex of a parabola to find the value of x.

questions.question: Find the maximum value of the function f(x) = 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x + 1 subject to the constraint x + 2y = 7, where x and y are both non-negative. (Round your answer to 2 decimal places)
questions.hint: Use the method of Lagrange multipliers or the simplex method to solve the problem.
